_comment: Third-party disk cleaners whose scheduled operations conflict with our storage module — if the user is already running CCleaner / Wise / BleachBit on a schedule, our sweeps will fight it for Prefetch entries + Delivery Optimization cache. If any listed installed-app / running-process / scheduled-task is detected, downgrade the storage module's default from 'auto' to 'warn_only' for the overlapping sources OR skip them entirely (per action). Analogous schema to explorer_conflicts.json.
_matchRule: OR across the three signals (installedAppNames / runningProcesses / scheduledTasks). Case-insensitive substring match.

conflicts.reason: CCleaner Smart Cleaning sweeps Prefetch + Temp on schedule. Overlapping cleanup wastes I/O and may race with theirs mid-run.
Wise Disk Cleaner's scheduled sweep covers the same targets as our storage module.
BleachBit is typically run interactively, not scheduled. Warn the user we'll also clean, but proceed.
Advanced SystemCare hooks into Prefetch cleanup and runs periodically.
Glary's 1-Click Maintenance touches the same targets.

defaultActionOnDetection.skip_prefetch_cleanup_and_delivery_optim: Do not touch %WINDIR%\Prefetch or the Delivery Optimization cache. Log the skip. The other storage sources (%TEMP%, Windows.old, DISM cleanup) still run.
defaultActionOnDetection.warn_only: Print a one-line notice to the user that another cleaner is installed and may re-clean the same paths. Proceed anyway.
